content.full_text:
TO: CENTRAL FILE
THRU: J. PRITCHARD
FROM: Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
RE: Code §§ and Regulations Referenced:

June 11, 1976

Mr. John W. Heckel, Secretary
Board of Law Library Trustees
191 North First Street
San Jose, California 95113

Dear Mr. Heckel:

This is in response to your letter of June 2, 1976, regarding the applicability of the Political Reform Act to your agency.

Government Code §87300 et seq. requires every state and local government agency to develop a Conflict of Interest Code. Government Code §82041, in pertinent part, defines "local government agency" as:

.... a county, city or district of any kind including school district, or any other local or regional political subdivision, or any department, division, bureau, office, board, commission or other agency of these ....

Boards of Law Library Trustees, established in each county and appointed in part by the County Board of Supervisors, pursuant to Business and Professions Code §6300 et seq., are local government agencies and, accordingly, are bound by the provisions of Government Code §87300 et seq.

Pursuant to the provisions of Government Code §82011, the code reviewing body for your agency is the Board of Supervisors of your county. That body should notify you of the deadline for submission of your Conflict of Interest Code. Should you require assistance in the preparation of your Code, please contact the County Counsel, who will supply you with the necessary information.

Sincerely,

Delbert L. Spurlock, Jr.
Chief, Conflicts of Interest Division

/glb
